title: Isolate MCP server capabilities
description: MCP servers are integrated with their advertised capabilities enforced; the client never assumes a capability the server did not declare.
body: Use when connecting an agent to an MCP server or exposing one. Do not call a tool or resource the server did not advertise, and avoid trusting server-returned content as instructions. Actions: read the server's declared capabilities, restrict the client to that set, validate tool schemas the server exposes, and treat returned resources as untrusted data. Validation signals include a test that an undeclared capability is not invoked and that a malicious server response cannot drive a privileged local action. Risk boundary: granting an external MCP server reach into side-effecting local tools without a gate is a critical finding.
